message:
feat: add project-memory agent to root agent tree

Global utility agent that manages per-project markdown memory files
via a bash CLI tool with list/read/write/search subcommands. Added
to root's agents list so all agents can delegate memory operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+---
+name: project-memory
+description: "Ask to manage project memory documents — list, read, write, or search .md files in the project memory directory"
+model: fast
+tools: []
+agents: []
+constraints:
+  max_turns: 20
+  max_depth: 0
+  can_spawn: false
+  can_learn: false
+  timeout_ms: 60000
+tags:
+  - core
+  - memory
+version: 1
+---
+
+You manage project memory documents using the memory-cli tool.
+
+Memory documents are .md files stored in the project's memory directory.
+Each document captures durable knowledge about the project — architecture
+decisions, conventions, patterns, or any information worth preserving
+across sessions.
+
+## Commands
+
+List all memory documents:
+
+    memory-cli list
+
+Read a specific document:
+
+    memory-cli read <filename>
+
+Write (create or overwrite) a document:
+
+    echo 'content' | memory-cli write <filename>
+
+Search across all documents:
+
+    memory-cli search <keyword>
+
+## Details
+
+**list** — Shows all .md files in the memory directory with each file's
+first line (title). Outputs nothing if no documents exist yet.
+
+**read** — Outputs the full contents of a memory file. The filename is
+just the basename (e.g. `architecture.md`), not a full path.
+
+**write** — Creates or overwrites a memory file. Content is read from
+stdin. The memory directory is created automatically if it doesn't exist.
+For multi-line content, use a heredoc:
+
+    cat <<'EOF' | memory-cli write architecture.md
+    # Architecture
+
+    This project uses a hexagonal architecture...
+    EOF
+
+**search** — Greps across all memory files for a keyword. Shows matching
+filenames and lines.
+
+## Role
+
+You are a data store. Execute the requested operation and report results
+clearly. When writing, confirm what was written. When reading, return the
+document contents. When listing, show all available documents. When
+searching, show all matches.

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+---
+name: memory-cli
+description: "Manage project memory files: list, read, write, search"
+interpreter: bash
+---
+#!/bin/bash
+TOOL_DIR="${SPROUT_TOOL_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}")" && pwd)}"
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+if [ -z "${SPROUT_PROJECT_DATA_DIR:-}" ]; then
+  echo "Error: SPROUT_PROJECT_DATA_DIR is not set" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+MEMORY_DIR="$SPROUT_PROJECT_DATA_DIR/memory"
+
+command="${1:-}"
+# consume the command arg; ok if no args remain
+shift 2>/dev/null || true
+
+case "$command" in
+  list)
+    if [ ! -d "$MEMORY_DIR" ]; then
+      echo "No memory directory yet. Write a document to create it."
+      exit 0
+    fi
+    found=0
+    for f in "$MEMORY_DIR"/*.md; do
+      [ -f "$f" ] || continue
+      found=1
+      name="$(basename "$f")"
+      title="$(head -n 1 "$f")"
+      printf "%-30s %s\n" "$name" "$title"
+    done
+    if [ "$found" -eq 0 ]; then
+      echo "No memory documents found."
+    fi
+    ;;
+
+  read)
+    filename="${1:-}"
+    if [ -z "$filename" ]; then
+      echo "Error: usage: memory-cli read <filename>" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    if [[ "$filename" == */* || "$filename" == ..* ]]; then
+      echo "Error: filename must be a simple basename (no path separators)" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    filepath="$MEMORY_DIR/$filename"
+    if [ ! -f "$filepath" ]; then
+      echo "Error: file not found: $filename" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    cat "$filepath"
+    ;;
+
+  write)
+    filename="${1:-}"
+    if [ -z "$filename" ]; then
+      echo "Error: usage: echo 'content' | memory-cli write <filename>" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    if [[ "$filename" == */* || "$filename" == ..* ]]; then
+      echo "Error: filename must be a simple basename (no path separators)" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    mkdir -p "$MEMORY_DIR"
+    filepath="$MEMORY_DIR/$filename"
+    cat > "$filepath"
+    echo "Wrote $filepath"
+    ;;
+
+  search)
+    keyword="${1:-}"
+    if [ -z "$keyword" ]; then
+      echo "Error: usage: memory-cli search <keyword>" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -d "$MEMORY_DIR" ]; then
+      echo "No memory directory yet. Nothing to search."
+      exit 0
+    fi
+    matches="$(grep -rl --include="*.md" "$keyword" "$MEMORY_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [ -z "$matches" ]; then
+      echo "No matches found."
+    else
+      echo "$matches" | while read -r f; do
+        name="$(basename "$f")"
+        echo "=== $name ==="
+        grep -n "$keyword" "$f"
+        echo ""
+      done
+    fi
+    ;;
+
+  *)
+    echo "Usage: memory-cli <command> [args]" >&2
+    echo "" >&2
+    echo "Commands:" >&2
+    echo "  list               List all memory documents" >&2
+    echo "  read <filename>    Read a memory document" >&2
+    echo "  write <filename>   Write a memory document (content from stdin)" >&2
+    echo "  search <keyword>   Search across all memory documents" >&2
+    exit 1
+    ;;
+esac

@@ -5,6 +5,7 @@ model: best
 tools: []
 agents:
   - utility/task-manager
+  - utility/project-memory
 constraints:
   max_turns: 200
   max_depth: 5
